name: Format Money
short_description: Format currency consistently as $ with two decimals and commas.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: The monetary value to be formatted

name: Format Percentage
short_description: Formats a numeric value into a string representing a percentage with two decimal places.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: Numeric value to format as a percentage

name: Net Profit Margin (Easy)
short_description: Calculates the Net Profit Margin given a company's revenue and net income.

inputs.name: revenue
inputs.description: Total revenue reported by the company.

name: Gross Profit Margin
short_description: Calculates Gross Profit Margin using revenue and cost of goods sold (COGS).

inputs.name: revenue
inputs.description: Total revenue generated by the company.

name: Operating Profit and Margin
short_description: Calculates Operating Profit (EBIT) and Operating Profit Margin from revenue, cost of goods sold (COGS), and operating expenses (Opex).

inputs.name: revenue
inputs.description: Total revenue or sales generated by the company.

name: Gross Profit Margin and Net Profit Margin
short_description: Calculates both the Gross Profit Margin and the Net Profit Margin based on revenue, cost of goods sold (COGS), and net income.

inputs.name: revenue
inputs.description: Total sales revenue generated by the company.

inputs.name: cogs
inputs.description: Cost of Goods Sold (direct costs attributable to the production of the goods or services sold by a company).

inputs.name: net_income
inputs.description: The profit of a company after all expenses, including taxes and interest, have been deducted from revenue.

name: Target Net Profit Margin Analysis
short_description: Compares current net profit margin against a higher target margin, calculating required net income and the additional net income needed.

inputs.name: target_margin
inputs.description: The desired net profit margin as a percentage (e.g., 25 for 25%).

name: Template Problem Generator
short_description: Utility function to generate multiple problem instances from various financial ratio templates and save them to a JSON file.

inputs.name: output_file
inputs.description: The file path where the generated problems will be saved.

inputs.name: num_instances
inputs.description: The number of problem instances to generate for each financial template.
